meaning: n.指计划,想法,创作等脑力劳动的创造物
examples: The record was the brainchild of rock star Bob Geldof.
The following year Jobs leaves his brainchild company.
Cadaver organ contracts are the brainchild of George Mason University economist and law professor Lloyd Cohen.
Additional competition for PayPal will come from Square, the brainchild of Twitter founder Jack Dorsey.
